name: Las Lagunas de Neila and ascent to Alto de la Campiña - circular through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space
name_original: Las Lagunas de Neila y subida al Alto de la Campiña — circular por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda
region_intro: Jogging in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila offers routes through a landscape characterized by glacial lakes, cirques, and peaks reaching over 2,000 meters. The region features extensive Scots pine forests, alongside beeches, oaks, and birch, providing varied terrain for runners. Alpine pastures and rocky sections are also present, contributing to the diverse running experience. The area's elevation changes and natural features make it suitable for trail running. Best jogging routes in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila The most popular jogging route is Lagunas de Neila Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the glacial lake landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Las Lagunas de Neila and ascent to Alto de la Campiña - circular through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space, a difficult 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route includes an ascent to Alto de la Campiña, offering panoramic views of the glacial lagoons. Local runners also love the Pico de la Laguna (2004m) – Neila Lagoons loop from Peña Aguda, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through high-altitude terrain and past glacial lakes, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Jogging in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila is defined by glacial lakes, pine forests, and mountain peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on more challenging trails. The routes in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 150 runners have used komoot to explore Lagunas Glaciares De Neila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila?
A: Lagunas Glaciares De Neila offers a selection of 18 running routes. The majority of these, 16 to be precise, are classified as difficult, providing a challenging experience for trail runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila?
A: The terrain in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila is highly varied, characterized by stunning glacial landscapes. Runners will encounter extensive Scots pine forests, alongside beeches, oaks, and birch. The routes often feature alpine pastures, rocky sections, and significant elevation changes, making it ideal for trail running.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050028450/las-lagunas-de-neila-and-ascent-to-alto-de-la-campina-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-demanda-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lagunas de Neila and ascent to Alto de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Laguna (2004m) – Neila Lagoons loop from Peña Aguda&lt;/a&gt;, taking you through high-altitude terrain and past glacial lakes.

Q: What are some natural features or landmarks I can see while running in the region?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see numerous glacial lakes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2010732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Neila Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3856363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cascada Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer breathtaking views from peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3856385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campiña&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Laguna (2004m)&lt;/a&gt;. The spectacular Las Calderas Gorge, with its crystalline pools and waterfalls, is another highlight.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila?
A: The park's beauty transforms with the seasons. Autumn offers vibrant colors, while winter presents a magical, frozen landscape, including impressive ice waterfalls at Laguna de la Cascada. For running, spring and early autumn generally provide the most pleasant conditions, avoiding the extremes of summer heat and heavy winter snows, though the area is accessible year-round for those prepared for the conditions.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier jog?
A: While the majority of routes are challenging, the region does offer shorter, lower-difficulty trails, typically around 2.5 km, that allow easier access to and enjoyment of the main lagoons. These are suitable for those seeking a less strenuous experience or for families.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while out on the trails?
A: Yes, Lagunas Glaciares De Neila is home to diverse fauna. Runners have the opportunity to observe wildlife such as wolves, deer, roe deer, wild boar, golden eagles, and otters, especially in quieter areas and during dawn or dusk.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila?
A: The running routes in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning glacial lake landscapes, the challenging elevation gains, and the panoramic views from peaks like Alto de la Campiña.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Lagunas Glaciares De Neila?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near the main access points to the glacial lagoons and trailheads. It's advisable to check specific access points or visitor information centers for detailed parking locations, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Are there any specific challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trail runners, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050026036/lagunas-de-neila-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Neila Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 8.1 km difficult route with significant elevation gain, provide a demanding experience. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064170909/sierra-al-pico-urbion-picacho-del-camperon-y-penas-blancas-trail-running-circular-por-el-parque-natural-laguna-negra-y-circos-glaciares-de-urbion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a circular trail running through the Natural Park Laguna Negra and Glacial Cirques of Urbión.

Q: Where can I find more information about the park's ecosystems and geology?
A: The Casa del Parque, located in the rehabilitated San Miguel church in Neila, serves as an information center. It provides helpful details about the park's ecosystems, geology, and fauna, which can be very useful for planning your running routes and understanding the natural environment.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the spectacular Las Calderas Gorge is known for its crystalline pools and waterfalls. Additionally, the Laguna de la Cascada boasts impressive ice waterfalls in winter, offering a unique sight for runners during the colder months.
